steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Recognize the standard form of a circle equation
steps.content: The equation $(x-3)^2 + (y+4)^2 = 144$ matches the standard form $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$. In this form, the number on the right side of the equation is $r^2$ (the radius squared), not the radius itself.

steps.title: Extract the radius from the equation
steps.content: From the equation, we have $r^2 = 144$. To find the radius, take the square root of both sides: $$r = \sqrt{144} = 12$$ The radius of the circle is 12.
